behavior_in_task_description.reason: instruction.md names all relevant files (commands.ts, program.ts, SKILL.md, session-management.md), the .playwright marker, cli.config.json path, and the renamed commands (install, session-kill-all).

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes what's broken and desired behavior without dictating exact code changes or function signatures.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: test_find_workspace_dir_function uses a regex to extract a function literally named 'findWorkspaceDir' from program.ts; a correct alternative fix using a different helper name (e.g., 'locateWorkspace' or inline logic) would fail the test even though behavior is equivalent.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests execute node scripts, run npm build/eslint, and invoke extracted code; not purely text grep.

test_not_tautological.reason: Tests assert specific command names present/absent and execute extracted workspace-finding logic against a real filesystem; stubs would fail.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Instruction requires updating SKILL.md and session-management.md documentation, but the visible tests don't clearly validate documentation updates (truncated, but listed assertions focus on code).

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are grouped by section comments (Gates, Pass-to-pass, Fail-to-pass) with descriptive names.

structured_data_schema.reason: Task is code refactoring, not structured data production.

no_typos.reason: Paths and command names in instruction.md are consistent and correctly spelled.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction points at relevant files which is appropriate given the task scope; doesn't name buggy lines.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: This is a code task, not an agentmd-edits/config task.
